essence: During YouTube WAI UAT David set the operating rule: code changes go through the SupportSignal repo with regular coding agents, workflow changes go through the Alex tool, and the workflow's shared data stays committed in git (never gitignored) because other users must synchronize it.

essence: David's 10-month-old MacBook Pro screen went interlaced then black; the local iCare shop repairs in-house (parts ordered from Singapore, ~7-10 days), AppleCare diagnostics are free with no accidental damage, a wipe is possible, so David is finishing a Time Machine backup before dropping it off.

actions.text: Bring the MacBook Pro in for AppleCare screen repair once backup completes

essence: Long David-Ian session crystallizing the AppyStack recipe model — composable prompt recipes (shell/persistence/Swagger) plus domain data-shapes that assemble micro-apps in an hour, with learnings folded back into the recipe — Ian advised to give KyberBot a recipe/extension area instead of baked-in extensions; they compared agent org-chart architecture (Samantha orchestrator, @-mention Slack discipline, 5-10 skills per specialist agent), David's agent-first team leverage (Jan/Mary via Loom handovers, Remotion/Nano Banana skills for AI-TLDR), the juice-shop bilingual farang/Thai-sty menu pipeline (markdown to JSON to NotebookLM infographic to Nano Banana 2 at ~12 cents), and the OpenClaw-aligned VC wave making reseller-consultants (Jess/Johan) and outside investment attractive, against a backdrop of both men's financial stress and David's visa exit around the 24th.

actions.text: Make a dedicated video featuring Ian's Kybernesis products rather than just mentions

essence: The Ansible coding profile still forces a three-profile selector on first load — hated as wasted energy since only one profile is ever wanted — so David asked for deep research on whether that capability has changed in the four months since it was last tested.

essence: Recording session rituals matter (mute notifications, route audio to headphones, prevent sleep) and a shared library/tooling layer would let David automate both Hammerspoon window layouts and automatic provisioning of Stream Deck buttons for recording setup.

David reframed Hammerspoon as a general window-layout/automation engine — commissioning deep research plus a dedicated brain (possibly a separate layout-engines brain) covering the old SupportSignal six-terminal layout file, Ecamm recording layouts (shorts/regular/livestream), multi-desktop use on the now-permanent M4 Pro, and app-launch automation like Storyline (cd, npm run dev, open browser) triggered from Ecamm, macOS Shortcuts, or a Stream Deck — while asking why the previous Hammerspoon layout attempt failed and whether Ansible can access his M4 Pro iTerm2 settings.
